creator: marcus@marcuscom.com
text:
On Tue, 2009-12-15 at 15:36 -0500, Greg Larkin wrote:
> -----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE-----
> Hash: SHA1
> 
> marcus@FreeBSD.org wrote:
> > Synopsis: repocopy databases/pear-Doctrine to databases/pear-Doctrine12
> > 
> > State-Changed-From-To: repocopy->open
> > State-Changed-By: marcus
> > State-Changed-When: Mon Dec 14 01:08:04 UTC 2009
> > State-Changed-Why: 
> > Repocopy complete.
> > 
> > 
> > Responsible-Changed-From-To: portmgr->miwi
> > Responsible-Changed-By: marcus
> > Responsible-Changed-When: Mon Dec 14 01:08:04 UTC 2009
> > Responsible-Changed-Why: 
> > Repocopy complete.
> > 
> > http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=141309
> 
> The repocopy created databases/pear-Doctrine2 instead of
> databases/pear-Doctrine12.
> 
> The Doctrine 2.x release is still alpha quality, and I don't plan to
> port that until it firms up.  I think it's OK to leave pear-Doctrine2
> around, but I'd still like pear-Doctrine12 for the 1.2.x release branch.
> 
> What do you think of that plan?

Sorry, I misread the PR.  I fixed it.

Joe

> 
> - -Greg
